electroacoustic.max_spl_note: Continuous maximum SPL at 1 m, measured in half-space with pink noise
electroacoustic.components: 12 in LF cone (4 in flat-wire copper voice coil) and 2 in exit HF compression driver (3 in titanium-alloy diaphragm) arranged as a single coaxial dual-concentric assembly sharing one magnet; the LF cone flare loads the HF driver

amplification.controller: Originally driven via the dedicated MTD 112 LLCa line-level controller (mono-amplification, one amp channel per enclosure); minimum 300 W into 8 ohms recommended (amplifier should sustain ~49 V continuously and ~60 V peak)
amplification.preset_note: FRONT = broadband FOH voicing; MONITOR = floor-monitor voicing; X-OVER = 110 Hz high-pass for use with a subwoofer

connection.input_connector: 1x 4-point speakON (NL4) per enclosure; wired pin 1+/1- (2+/2- unused), single-channel passive feed from one amplifier output
connection.breakouts: Controller line-level I/O (when used with the MTD 112 LLCa) is on 3-pin XLR, balanced; a separate mono SUB/SUM output feeds subwoofer or central-source amplification

physical.cabinet: 18 mm Baltic birch plywood, rabbeted/screwed/glued, with internal steel bracing; structured heavy-duty paint in RAL 8019 maroon-gray; honeycomb perforated 1.5 mm steel grille with reticulated foam

rigging_accessories: ETR1 adjustable U-bracket (primary flown/wall/scaffold mount, horizontal orientation only; WLL 27.5 daN, safety factor >12)
PION1 Aeroquip flying stud (secondary safety attachment only, not a main rigging point)
deployment.rigging_note: Three Aeroquip-type fixing posts (top, bottom, rear) plus four threaded fixing nuts for accessory or U-bracket mounting; flown suspension is horizontal-only via the ETR1

usage: Compact 12 in/2 in coaxial point source for small-to-medium FOH, monitoring, side-fill, and delay/underbalcony duty; its single-axis coherence makes it well suited to wedge use in any orientation.

Each cabinet is passive and requires exactly one amplifier channel; budget 4 enclosures per LA4 and aim for at least ~300 W into 8 ohms to keep the amp out of clipping (the controller's limiters handle driver protection).
Originally a system with the MTD 112 LLCa controller: pick FRONT for broadband FOH, MONITOR for stage wedges, and X-OVER (110 Hz high-pass) when subs are added.
For FOH it works best as a true point source - deploy singly or in a tight pair, ideally with delay-compensated multiple sources rather than large arrays.

Sub pairing: roughly one MTD 118 per pair of MTD112b, or one SB 218 for four MTD112b, fed mono from the controller's summed SUB output; keep the sub-to-top path-length offset under ~1.7 m to avoid crossover cancellation.
Discontinued legacy MTD product - flown installs are horizontal-only via the ETR1 bracket, with the PION1 stud serving only as a secondary safety point.
source_manual: MTD 112 Owner Manual (EN) and MTD112b CE Declaration of Conformity (EN)
